_meta.register: B2B owner-operator — direct, plain English, contractor-to-contractor respect. Layman's terms. No agency jargon. Speak to the roofing-company owner like a peer who studied their site, not a pitch deck.
_meta.math_style: narrative — '100 clicks gets you 2-3 inquiries on a roofing site, low-thousands per real quote' — NEVER token-concatenation
_meta.leak_anchor_rule: Always reference a real leak from {leak_1} or {leak_2}. Never invent issues.

A.label: Heritage / BBB / Trust-Underused
A.thesis: The roofer's biggest trust asset (years in business, BBB, GAF cert) is buried — and paid traffic is leaking. Direct hook on the specific underused trust asset.

beats.title: Permission-Based Open
beats.line: Hey, this is {caller} — I know I'm calling out of the blue, mind if I take 30 seconds and you can tell me to hang up after?
beats.followup: 30 seconds, then you decide.
beats.note: Permission-based open beats every cold-call gimmick. Tone: calm peer, not eager seller. Smile through the phone.

beats.title: Name The Specific Leak
beats.line: I run conversion audits on roofing company websites — and I pulled up {company} this morning. Real quick: {leak_1}. Did I read that right?
beats.followup: Quick — I'm Zack with RME, we work with roofing contractors on what's leaking before they spend another dime on Google Ads.
beats.note: Read the real leak from {leak_1}. Don't paraphrase. The specificity is the credibility.

beats.title: Roofing Money Math (Narrative)
beats.line: Here's why that matters in {market} — roofing clicks run about ${cpc_low} to ${cpc_high} a click on Google. About 100 clicks gets you maybe 2 or 3 real inquiries on a good roofing page. So every weak homepage piece is costing you in the low thousands per actual quote — not pennies.
beats.followup: Even if you only do LSAs or referrals — every page-view that doesn't call is still a lost shot.
beats.note: Narrative math only. No decimal-precise dollar quotes. Use 'low-thousands per real quote' or 'a few thousand per booked job'.

beats.title: Second Leak Punch
beats.line: And I saw another piece too — {leak_2}. You add that on top of the first one and most of your homepage visitors are bouncing before they even see your phone number.
beats.followup: I'm talking about above-the-fold, mobile, in the first 3 seconds — that's where most homeowners decide.
beats.note: Stack two real leaks before the offer. Two specific facts beat one big claim every time.

beats.title: The Free Audit Offer
beats.line: Here's why I'm calling — I built a free Roofing Site Conversion Audit. It's about a $3,000 piece of work, real numbers from your homepage, your competitors in {market}, and the 3 to 5 specific things to fix first to turn more visits into booked inspections. No pitch on the call — you get the audit, you do what you want with it.
beats.followup: No catch — I do 5 a week, free, because they turn into clients about 1 in 5 times. Worth my time even if not yours yet.
beats.note: Audit framed as a peer giving you a teardown — not an agency pitch. Be matter-of-fact about why it's free.

beats.title: Soft Booking
beats.line: I can have it on your desk in 48 hours — what's the best email to send it to, and is there a 20-minute window this week or next we can walk it together if you have questions?
beats.followup: Totally — even no walk-through, I'll send the PDF and you can read it solo.
beats.note: Always offer the optional walk-through but accept email-only. Don't push the meeting if they push back.

beats.title: Confirm + Set Expectation
beats.line: Got it — {company} audit going to that email. I'll text you when it lands so you know to look for it. Anything specific you want me to look at first?
beats.followup: Cool — I'll dig into that piece too.
beats.note: Confirm the email back to them out loud. Catches typos and signals you actually care.

beats.title: Email-Only Path
beats.line: Cool — what email is best? I'll send the PDF, and if anything in it sparks a question just reply.
beats.followup: Totally fine — I'll grab the contact from your contact page.
beats.note: Some owners hate meetings. Honor it. The audit does the selling.

B.label: Storm / Hail / Insurance-Claim Frame
B.thesis: In hail-belt markets, the biggest leak is no storm or insurance language above the fold. Hits hardest in TX, OK, CO, KS. Lead with the seasonal money window.

beats.title: Storm-Frame Open
beats.line: Hey, this is {caller} — quick one. You guys roof in {market} right? Cool. I run conversion audits for roofing companies and I noticed something on {company} that's costing you during hail season — got 60 seconds?
beats.followup: 60 seconds, then you tell me to keep going or hang up.
beats.note: Storm hook only works in hail-belt markets — check {market} before delivering. If it's CA or PNW, route to variant A or C.

beats.title: The Insurance Buyer Gap
beats.line: When a homeowner gets a hail event they're searching things like 'insurance roof claim' and 'storm damage repair {market}'. I pulled your site this morning and the issue I saw is — {leak_2}. So your paid clicks during storm season are landing on a page that doesn't speak to that buyer at all.
beats.followup: Even if you don't chase storms — the storm buyer is the buyer with the deductible already approved. Big check.
beats.note: Insurance-claim buyer has a 5-figure check waiting. Make sure the rep hears this — it's the highest LTV roofing buyer.

beats.title: Storm Click Cost
beats.line: Storm and hail-restoration keywords in {market} run about ${cpc_low} to ${cpc_high} a click — sometimes higher during active storm weeks. About 100 clicks gets you 2 or 3 inquiries on a clean page. So if your above-the-fold doesn't say 'we work your insurance claim,' you're losing a few thousand dollars in ad spend per insurance job that walks.
beats.followup: Even on LSAs and referrals — the same buyer reads the same homepage.
beats.note: Roofing CPCs spike during active storm weeks. Use this fact, don't make it up. Narrative math only.

beats.title: Stack The Trust Leak
beats.line: And second piece — {leak_1}. Homeowner who's nervous about a hail claim wants to see GAF certified, BBB, photos of the crew, real years in business — fast. If that's not above the fold they bounce.
beats.followup: It's the homeowner gut-check moment.
beats.note: Reference the actual {leak_1} — usually heritage/BBB/cert is the trust asset that's buried.

beats.title: Audit Offer
beats.line: Here's why I'm calling — I do a free Roofing Site Conversion Audit. About a $3,000 piece of work normally — your homepage, your competitors in {market}, your storm and insurance positioning, and the 3 to 5 fixes that move the needle first. No pitch on the call. You get the audit, you decide.
beats.followup: No catch. About 1 in 5 turn into clients later — that's enough for me to keep doing them.
beats.note: Be matter-of-fact. The audit IS the offer. The relationship is the side effect.

beats.title: Email + Window
beats.line: Best email to send it to? And if you want a 20-minute walk-through this week I can do that — totally optional.
beats.followup: Either way. 48 hours.
beats.note: Optional walk-through. Don't push it.

beats.title: Confirm
beats.line: {company} audit, 48 hours, going to that email. Anything specific about storm season or insurance positioning you want me to focus on?
beats.followup: Got it — I'll bake it in.
beats.note: Echo email back. Catch typos.

beats.title: Email Only
beats.line: Cool — what email? Reply if anything sparks a question, totally optional.
beats.followup: Easy.
beats.note: Honor the email-only path.

C.label: Competitor / Routing / CTA Direct
C.thesis: For prospects with strong CTAs but specific leaks (mobile, routing, residential/commercial split, reviews not above fold). Direct peer-to-peer hook. Use for Tier 3 and Tier 4 conversations.

beats.title: Peer Direct Open
beats.line: Hey — this is {caller}, RME. Quick question, you have 30 seconds? I'm not selling anything, I want to tell you something I caught on {company}.
beats.followup: 30 seconds — promise.
beats.note: Direct, no permission-loop padding. For prospects that already look strong — they'll respect directness.

beats.title: Specific Surgical Leak
beats.line: Pulled your homepage this morning. Two things — {leak_1}. And then — {leak_2}. Real fixable, but they're costing you on every paid click and every referral that lands on your site.
beats.followup: Even if you know — most contractors haven't priced what it's costing them. That's the part most people miss.
beats.note: Lead with two stacked leaks. No fluff. These are stronger prospects so they'll dismiss generic claims fast.

beats.title: Cost-Of-Inaction Math (Narrative)
beats.line: Roofing clicks in {market} run about ${cpc_low} to ${cpc_high} on Google. About 100 clicks gets you 2 or 3 real inquiries on a clean page. So those two leaks together are quietly costing you a few thousand dollars per booked job — without you ever seeing where the money went.
beats.followup: Same math holds on LSAs and referrals — the homepage either converts or it doesn't.
beats.note: Cost-of-inaction is the lever. Don't quote dollar-per-quote like an equation. Narrative.

beats.title: Free Audit Offer
beats.line: Here's why I'm calling — I do a free Roofing Site Conversion Audit. About $3,000 of work normally. Your homepage, your top 3 competitors in {market}, the 3 to 5 specific things to fix first. No pitch on the call. You get the audit, you decide.
beats.followup: No catch — I do 5 a week, about 1 in 5 turn into clients. That's enough math for me.
beats.note: Repeat the offer cleanly. Don't sell, deliver.

beats.title: Book Or Email
beats.line: What's the best email to send it to? Audit's in 48 hours. If you want a 20-minute walk-through this week I can do that, totally optional.
beats.followup: Either path works.
beats.note: Soft close, optional walk-through.

beats.title: Confirm + Hook
beats.line: Cool — {company} audit, 48 hours, going to that email. I'll text you when it lands. Anything in particular you want me to focus on?
beats.followup: Got it.
beats.note: Echo email. Always. Catch typos.

beats.title: Email Only
beats.line: Cool — what email? Reply if anything sparks a question.
beats.followup: Easy.
beats.note: Honor email-only path.
